rules.reason: Geographic movement data with real-time updates requires an interactive map with AIS layers and position overlays.
rules.interaction: Pan/zoom map, MMSI lookup, live position refresh

rules.reason: Historical position data is best shown as a geographic track with route replay for temporal analysis.
Route progress combines geographic and temporal dimensions — a route map with ETA and leg timeline is required.
rules.interaction: Click legs for detail, hover for ETA/distance remaining

rules.reason: Progress percentage and distance remaining are KPI-style temporal metrics best paired with a voyage tracker.
Geofencing is inherently geographic — zones must render as map overlays with breach indicators.
rules.interaction: Toggle zone layers, alert on boundary crossing

rules.reason: Tank fill levels are comparative KPIs over time — vertical gauges with consumption sparklines enable at-a-glance monitoring.
rules.interaction: Hover tank for days-remaining detail, trend sparkline

rules.reason: Historical trends require line or area charts to reveal patterns, anomalies, and rate of change.
rules.interaction: Brush time range, hover crosshair for values

rules.reason: Fleet-wide KPI matrices reveal outliers — heatmaps make underperforming vessels visible at a glance.
rules.interaction: Hover for vessel/metric values, color scale legend

rules.reason: Scalar KPIs with targets need compact comparison — bullet charts show actual vs target with qualitative bands.
rules.interaction: Hover qualitative ranges, compare actual vs target

rules.reason: Safety events are temporal sequences — a timeline reveals incident clustering and escalation patterns.
rules.interaction: Chronological scrub, filter by severity, acknowledge inline

rules.reason: Crew roster data is hierarchical list data with status badges — a certification panel beats a chart.
rules.interaction: Filter by expiry, sort by role, status traffic-light

rules.reason: Machinery sensor readings are real-time scalars — radial or bar gauges with threshold bands are standard bridge UX.
rules.interaction: Hover for live value, color threshold alerts

rules.reason: Wind direction and speed are vector data — a wind rose reveals dominant headings that scalar cards cannot.
rules.interaction: Hover sectors for speed/frequency, toggle Beaufort scale

rules.reason: Vessel health is categorical state data — traffic-light status cards communicate operational readiness instantly.
Operational workflows are sequential — step diagrams or ordered checklists show process state better than charts.
Hierarchical relationships require tree or nested list layouts to preserve parent-child context.
Real-time telemetry streams need a live-updating panel with sparklines rather than static snapshots.
